result:
# Introducing Smart Drafts

We've all felt it — that pause when you open a new document, cursor blinking, unsure where to begin. The blank page is one of the most consistent frictions we've heard about from you, and it shows up everywhere: meeting notes, project briefs, customer follow-ups, quick memos. You know what you want to say; you just need a running start. Today, we're giving you one.

Smart Drafts turns any new document into a first draft you can shape, rather than a blank canvas you have to fill. Give your document a title, click *Suggest Outline*, and we'll generate a structured starting point based on the title and the context of your recent work — the projects you've been in, the docs you've been writing, the vocabulary your team already uses. It's designed to feel like a thoughtful colleague handing you a napkin sketch: close enough to be useful, loose enough to rewrite. Everything stays fully editable, and nothing is shared outside your workspace.

This is the first step in a larger bet on writing that feels like *yours*. Next up is custom voice training, which will let Smart Drafts learn the cadence, structure, and tone of how you and your team actually write — so the outlines we suggest sound less like a generic assistant and more like the person sitting next to you. We'll have more to share on that soon. In the meantime, open a new doc and give Smart Drafts a try. As always, we'd love to hear what you think.

The blank page is one of the most consistent frictions we've heard about from you, and it shows up everywhere: meeting notes, project briefs, customer follow-ups, quick memos. You know what you want to say; you just need a running start. Today, we're giving you one.

Smart Drafts turns any new document into a first draft you can shape, rather than a blank canvas you have to fill. Give your document a title, click *Suggest Outline*, and we'll generate a structured starting point based on the title and the context of your recent work — the projects you've been in, the docs you've been writing, the vocabulary your team already uses. It's designed to feel like a thoughtful colleague handing you a napkin sketch: close enough to be useful, loose enough to rewrite. Everything stays fully editable, and nothing is shared outside your workspace.

This is the first step in a larger bet on writing that feels like *yours*. Next up is custom voice training, which will let Smart Drafts learn the cadence, structure, and tone of how you and your team actually write — so the outlines we suggest sound less like a generic assistant and more like the person sitting next to you. We'll have more to share on that soon. In the meantime, open a new doc and give Smart Drafts a try. As always, we'd love to hear what you think.
